data.title: Biggest stock movers: AVGO, LUNR, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher in premarket trading on Friday as investors awaited Fed Chair Jerome Powell's speech, following market swings driven by President Donald Trump’s tariff policy shifts.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers

Biggest stock gainers

  • Walgreens Boots (NASDAQ:WBA) +5% – Shares gained after private equity firm Sycamore Partners agreed to acquire the company for $10B. Sycamore will pay $11.45 per share, with shareholders potentially receiving an additional $3 from proceeds tied to the sale of Walgreens' primary-care assets, including VillageMD. The total transaction value could reach $23.7B. Expected to close in Q4, the deal includes a 35-day “go-shop” period, allowing Walgreens to seek alternative offers. Sycamore has secured fully committed financing, and the transaction is not subject to a financing condition.
  • Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) +13% – Shares soared after the semiconductor and software giant posted strong FQ1 results and guidance, reinforcing that AI-related spending remains robust. CEO Hock Tan highlighted continued strength in AI semiconductor revenue, projecting $4.4B in Q2 as hyperscale partners ramp up investments in AI XPUs and connectivity solutions for AI data centers. For Q2, Broadcom forecasts revenue of approximately $14.9B, surpassing the $14.59B consensus, with adjusted EBITDA expected to be around 66% of total revenue.
  • The Gap (NYSE:GAP) +18% – Shares jumped as the retailer surpassed Q4 estimates, fueled by better-than-expected comparable sales in three of its four brands and its strongest gross margin in two decades. Despite the strong results, Gap issued cautious guidance for the current quarter, citing macroeconomic uncertainty and potential effects of U.S. trade policy, though less than 1% of its merchandise comes from Mexico and Canada. For Q1, net sales are projected to be flat to slightly higher than Q1 2024’s $3.4B, with a modest Y/Y gross margin increase from 41.2%. In FY25, net sales are expected to rise 1%–2% to $15.25B–$15.40B, in line with the $15.38B consensus, while operating income is forecast to be between $1.20B and $1.22B.

Biggest stock losers

  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) -20% – Shares plunged after the company posted mixed FQ1 results and issued a weaker-than-expected outlook. HPE also announced a cost-reduction program, including a workforce reduction of up to 3,000 employees. For the current quarter, the company expects adjusted EPS between $0.28 and $0.34, significantly below the $0.48 consensus, with projected revenue of $7.2B–$7.6B, missing the $7.94B estimate. For FY2025, HPE forecasts adjusted EPS with a midpoint of $1.80, falling short of the $2.12 consensus.
  • Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) -34% – Shares plunged after reports of complications with its IM-2 mission Nova-C lunar lander, Athena, despite maintaining a signal with the spacecraft. The company has been reallocating power to critical systems to address unexpected positioning issues on the moon, raising investor concerns about the mission's success and its broader implications. With a press conference scheduled for later today, the market awaits further details on Athena’s status and next steps. The setback has dampened optimism about Intuitive Machines’ space exploration capabilities, fueling uncertainty about its valuation and future prospects.

data.title: Faraday Future changes its ticker symbol from FFIE to FFAI
data.description:
Faraday Future Intelligent (NASDAQ:FFIE) announced that the ticker symbol for its Class A Common Stock will officially change from "FFIE" to "FFAI" on Monday, March 10. In addition, the ticker symbol for the company’s warrants will change from "FFIEW" to "FFAIW."

Simultaneously, FF is making significant strides in its All-AI Mobility Ecosystem, with its advanced AI-driven in-vehicle system nearing completion. The company plans to bolster its AI development by expanding its R&D efforts and hiring new AI talent.

Effective March 10, 2025, the company will change its stock ticker to "FFAI," reflecting its focus on AI. This change does not affect operations or require shareholder action.

data.title: U.S. plans to levy fees on China-linked ships, push allies to do same - report
data.description:
The U.S. is planning to impose significant fees on ships linked to China, including those built in China or flying the Chinese flag, and will also push allies to do the same, or they could face retaliation, Reuters reported, citing a draft executive order. 

The U.S. intends to charge fees of up to $1.5 million for docking at its ports on any ship that is part of a fleet containing Chinese-built or Chinese-flagged vessels. The draft executive order, dated February 27, is an attempt by the Trump administration to boost domestic shipbuilding and reduce China's hold on the global shipping industry.

The fee is based on a U.S. Trade Representative's office proposal last month to levy fees on Chinese vessels; however, a key difference is that the draft executive order does not state that port fees would be imposed when Chinese-built ships account for 25% or more of vessels operating, slated for delivery, or on order.

Any vessel entering a U.S. port should be subject to fees "regardless of where it was built or flagged, if that vessel is part of a fleet that includes vessels built or flagged in the PRC (People's Republic of China)," the report said.

The U.S. plans to encourage its allies to adopt similar measures, with potential consequences for those that do not comply. However, the specific allies targeted by this initiative are not yet clearly defined.

With the primary goal to bolster U.S. shipbuilding capabilities and diminish China's dominance in the maritime, logistics, and shipping sectors, such fees could significantly increase freight costs for U.S. exporters, potentially affecting their competitiveness in the global market.

Related tickers to watch: Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF), Matson (MATX), Star Bulk Carriers (SBLK), ZIM Intergrated Shipping Services (ZIM), Golden Ocean Group (GOGL), Danaos (DAC) Breakwave Dry Bulk Shipping ETF (NYSEARCA:BDRY), SonicShares Global Shipping ETF (NYSEARCA:BOAT), U.S. Global Sea to Sky Cargo ETF (NYSEARCA:SEA).

data.title: Hunting PLC acquires Organic Oil Recovery Technology for $17.5M
European markets dip after a volatile week
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.59% to 8,631. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.64% to 23,035.

France (CAC:IND) -1.11% to 8,104. France imports and exports remained stable in January.

Slovakia’s economy advanced by 1.8% Y/Y in Q4.

Industrial production in Spain shrank 1% Y/Y in January.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.98% lower to 550.6, rounding off a volatile week due to ongoing uncertainty surrounding US trade policy under the Trump administration, which continued to weigh on investor sentiment. Luxury stocks were among the worst performers.

The European Central Bank made the anticipated interest rate cut  but took a more cautious approach to future rate changes while raising its near-term inflation forecasts.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area GDP expected shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.25%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 7 basis points to 2.82%.

UK's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.67%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: SpaceX's Starship explodes after launch again, debris disrupts Florida flights
data.description:
SpaceX's (SPACE) Starship exploded in space minutes after takeoff on Thursday, marking the second unsuccessful test in a row for the massive rocket, as the explosion scattered debris over parts of the Caribbean.

"During Starship's ascent burn, the vehicle experienced a rapid unscheduled disassembly and contact was lost," the Elon Musk-owned company said.

SpaceX (SPACE) later said an "energetic event" in the rocket's aft portion resulted in the loss of several Raptor engines. "This in turn led to a loss of attitude control and ultimately a loss of communications with Starship."

The Federal Aviation Administration "briefly slowed aircraft outside the area where space vehicle debris was falling or stopped aircraft at their departure location." Normal operations have since resumed.

The explosion led to flight disruptions at Florida's busiest airports, as well as flight diversions around the Turks and Caicos Islands. SpaceX (SPACE) said the debris did not contain toxic materials. 

The FAA directed SpaceX (SPACE) to carry out a "mishap investigation" into the incident. "We will conduct a thorough investigation, in coordination with the FAA, and implement corrective actions," the company said.

Meanwhile, the Super Heavy booster separated from Starship's upper stage and returned to Earth as planned, where it was caught by the mechanical arms of "Mechazilla" - the 400-ft rocket-catching structure at Starbase in Texas. This marked the third successful catch of a Super Heavy booster.

data.title: France imports and exports remained stable in January
data.description:
France's trade balance remains at -€5.6B in January 2025 after three months of improvement. Over the month, exports and imports were stable: €50.7B for exports and €56.3B for imports.The energy balance deteriorated slightly (-€0.1B) after a continuous improvement since June 2024. The balance of manufactured products, driven by exports, records a slight increase which offsets the decrease in the energy balance (+€0.1B).The balance of capital goods continues to improve (+€0.2 B after +€0.4B in December 2024). The balance of intermediate goods decreases (-€0.1B) and that of consumer goods remains at its December 2024 level.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)

Currency: (EUR:USD)
